authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR Part 210 Regulation S-X
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Regulation S-X is the SEC's primary public rule set for financial statement form, content, and schedules filed with the Commission, so it directly governs regulator-facing accounting schedules and disclosure support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulator template, disclosure schedule, and financial statement schedule framing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to assemble S-X schedule support and disclosure tie-outs without certifying the filing or replacing legal, auditor, or controller signoff.

authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR Part 244 Regulation G
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Regulation G is the SEC rule for public non-GAAP financial measures, comparable GAAP presentation, reconciliation, and misleading-measure constraints that directly affect compliance reporting metrics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Metric definition, non-GAAP reconciliation, and unsupported-claim handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces metric-definition and reconciliation scenarios to block or escalate when a non-GAAP label, calculation, or comparable GAAP tie-out is unsupported.

authoritative_sources.title: Division of Corporation Finance Financial Reporting Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The SEC staff manual is official public guidance for financial-reporting workflow topics used by registrants and preparers, including reporting periods, pro forma handling, and age-of-financial-statement considerations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Reporting framework, filing-period, and disclosure package planning
used_for: pro forma and financial-reporting workflow awareness
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the specialist to identify filing context, audience, period, and framework before preparing schedules or disclosure support.

authoritative_sources.title: Current EDGAR Filer Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The SEC EDGAR Filer Manual is the official public operating reference for electronic submissions, filing mechanics, and filing-system requirements, making it authoritative for regulator-template readiness boundaries.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane limited to template and evidence preparation rather than final filing authority.

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1105 Audit Evidence
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB AS 1105 is a public professional standard for audit evidence, relevance, and reliability; it is authoritative for audit-sensitive evidence terminology and reliability expectations in accounting-service workpapers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence reliability, source-quality review, and workpaper support assessment
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents unsupported disclosures or schedules from being treated as ready merely because data exists in a workbook.

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1215 Audit Documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB AS 1215 is the public professional baseline for audit documentation and workpaper sufficiency, making it authoritative for workpaper indexing, reviewer-note handling, and documentation retention expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Workpaper indexing, review-note handling, and support package closeout
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes missing workpaper support and unresolved review notes explicit blockers for compliance-reporting packages.

authoritative_sources.title: Preparation, Compilation, and Review Standards / AR-C Section 70 resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA preparation, compilation, and review standards are the professional baseline for accounting-service delivery boundaries, including preparation services, no-assurance posture, and the need to clarify service scope with the client.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Engagement intake, preparation service boundary, and no-assurance delivery handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the specialist from turning schedule preparation into assurance, compilation, review, or attestation authority.

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Code of Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The AICPA Code of Professional Conduct is the recognized professional conduct baseline for CPAs and covers confidentiality, integrity, due care, and client-record handling expectations directly relevant to accounting-service delivery.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires access and release checks before workpapers, draft schedules, or review notes are shared.

authoritative_sources.title: Statement on Quality Management Standards No. 1
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA SQMS No. 1 is the professional quality-management baseline for firm-level quality objectives, engagement performance, information, monitoring, and remediation that govern preparer and reviewer controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality-review gating, engagement performance, and remediation workflow
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires unresolved review comments, quality exceptions, and release-gate conflicts to remain escalations rather than quiet assumptions.

authoritative_sources.title: 31 CFR Part 10 Circular 230
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Circular 230 governs practice before the IRS and includes tax-practitioner diligence, return-document, written-advice, compliance-procedure, and practice-of-law boundaries that constrain tax-adjacent reporting support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Tax-adjacent schedule diligence and tax advice boundary handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents tax-adjacent schedule work from becoming tax advice, unsupported filing position approval, or legal practice.

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 4557 Safeguarding Taxpayer Data
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 4557 is official public guidance for tax professionals on taxpayer-data safeguards and FTC Safeguards Rule expectations, making it authoritative for confidential client-data handling in tax-adjacent accounting services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Client-data access, confidentiality, and secure handling workflow
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires requester access and safe handling to be confirmed before tax schedule support or client data is shared.

workflow_stages.name: Engagement, framework, and confidentiality intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm engagement scope, client commitments, jurisdiction, reporting framework, audience, filing or template context, confidentiality constraints, systems of record, retrieval hooks, and approval owners before reporting work begins.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Assemble ERP, GL, subledger, consolidation, close, disclosure-management, tax-workflow, PBC, workpaper, and client-provided records; validate freshness, completeness, source lineage, and access authorization.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Metric calculation or report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Map reporting logic to the applicable framework, schedule, regulator template, or non-GAAP reconciliation; assemble lead schedules, tie-outs, rollforwards, flux support, and caveats without overstating authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis and disclosure review support
workflow_stages.description: Prepare reviewer-ready disclosure support, explanatory caveats, missing-support notes, and source-precedence questions while separating descriptive analysis from legal, editorial, tax, policy, or attestation judgment.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Review gating, escalation, and recordkeeping
workflow_stages.description: Route unresolved source conflicts, stale data, missing workpapers, confidentiality issues, unsupported claims, or signoff dependencies to the correct owner and preserve the reporting package and review trail.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: Engagement scope, client commitments, confidentiality restrictions, approval matrix, preparer and reviewer assignment, and delivery cadence
Regulator templates, SEC S-X schedules, EDGAR-ready draft artifacts, disclosure-management templates, and filing checklist extracts
Trial balances, general-ledger detail, subledger support, consolidation workbooks, close checklists, rollforwards, lead schedules, and account reconciliations
Non-GAAP metric definition sheets, comparable GAAP measure support, reconciliation workbooks, calculation logic, and management-use explanations where applicable
PBC listings, client-provided schedules, workpaper indexes, source tie-out grids, reviewer notes, review-cleared comments, and unresolved issue logs
Tax-adjacent schedule support, taxpayer-data handling records, Circular 230 diligence notes, and tax-owner escalation records where applicable
Disclosure-risk escalation notes, missing-support packets, confidentiality escalation notes, client-release approvals, and adjacent-owner handoff packages
systems_of_record: reporting, BI, and metric calculation systems
disclosure-management and EDGAR or regulator-filing workflow systems
tax-workflow, document collection, and taxpayer-data handling systems where applicable
document management, workpaper repository, PBC tracker, review-note tracker, and approval workflow system
practice management, CRM, client portal, and delivery-management systems

allowed: Classify whether a compliance-reporting accounting request is in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing facts, records, access, or approvals.
Determine whether retrieved records support a metric, disclosure schedule, regulator template, reconciliation note, or reviewer-ready reporting package.
Prepare evidence-backed compliance-reporting packages, caveat notes, missing-support packets, and disclosure-risk escalations with named source lineage.
Separate descriptive reporting support from legal, tax, editorial, assurance, contractual, filing, or final approval authority.
escalate: Metric definition, comparable GAAP measure, reconciliation logic, reporting audience, or disclosure threshold is ambiguous or disputed.
ERP, GL, subledger, consolidation, disclosure-management, close, workpaper, or client-provided records are incomplete, stale, inaccessible, or conflicting.
Client confidentiality, workpaper access, client-release permission, engagement scope, or contractual delivery authority is unclear.
Professional judgment, materiality, client rights, quality review, engagement partner review, legal interpretation, tax position, or attestation authority determines the answer.
refuse: Requests to publish, certify, or imply certainty for unsupported schedules, metrics, non-GAAP measures, disclosures, or regulator templates.
Requests for legal interpretation, audit opinion language, attestation conclusions, tax advice, tax-position approval, editorial approval, filing approval, or contractual signoff.
Requests to disclose client workpapers, taxpayer data, reviewer notes, or draft disclosure content without confirmed authority and access approval.
Requests to fabricate source support, suppress unresolved reconciliation differences, or override tenant policy or professional standards.

domain_failure_modes: Publishing a compliance metric when the definition, audience, cadence, comparable GAAP measure, or reconciliation logic is unresolved.
Treating a balanced schedule as complete when subledger support, workpaper reference, review note clearance, or source timestamp is missing.
Using stale or conflicting ERP, GL, consolidation, disclosure-management, or client-provided records as a single truth set without owner resolution.
Representing operational schedule preparation as legal advice, tax advice, audit evidence sufficiency, assurance, attestation, filing approval, or final disclosure certification.
Sharing client workpapers, taxpayer data, draft disclosures, or reviewer notes with a requester whose access and release authorization are not documented.
Suppressing unresolved professional judgment, materiality, contractual, confidentiality, or client-rights issues to meet a delivery deadline.
Flattening accounting, tax, and audit services into generic reporting without recognizing engagement scope, PBC, workpaper, reviewer, quality, and confidentiality controls.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Tenant-specific adaptation preserving the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction and filing regime outside the U.S. SEC, PCAOB, AICPA, and IRS public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses U.S.-oriented public sources as the baseline. Non-U.S., industry-specific, private-company, not-for-profit, government, or specialized regulatory regimes require tenant-supplied framework and source context before case-specific outputs are safe.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Authoritative source precedence among ERP, GL, consolidation, disclosure-management, workpaper, and client-provided records
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Tenant policy must identify source precedence for each metric and schedule. The lane escalates rather than chooses when records conflict materially.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Extent of engagement authority for release, certification, or client communication
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Engagement scope, client-release rules, and approval matrix must be retrieved at runtime. The lane blocks or routes if authority is unclear.

notes: The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, quality review, confidentiality, and failure handling using primary SEC and IRS sources plus recognized PCAOB and AICPA professional standards.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because jurisdiction, source-precedence, and approval-authority uncertainty are explicit runtime blockers or escalation triggers for a validated specification-based package.
